Extract and structure the following information as valid JSON only (no markdown, no extra text).Return ONLY this JSON structure:{"summary": "2-3 sentence summary of the video content","keyPoints": ["point 1", "point 2", ...],"aiConcepts": ["AI concept 1", "AI concept 2", ...],"technicalDetails": ["tool/framework 1", "implementation detail", ...],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 120, "reason": "Code demo", "context": "Shows Python implementation"}]}If the video is not about AI/tech, set aiConcepts and technicalDetails to empty arrays but still extract keyPoints.TRANSCRIPT:what's going on everybody Josh pook here and in today's video we're going to be taking a look at another AI agent tool this tool is called GPT me this agent lives in your terminal equipped with local tools can write code use the terminal browse the web and has Vision capabilities it's self-correcting and you can get it up and running within just one to two prompts we're going to talk about how you can set it up use it and how it also compares to some of the other AI agent tools we've covered on this channel such as agent Q agent e open interpreter and more let's dive right into [Music] it all right so a lot of you guys know that we've covered agent Q on this channel we also covered agent e also we've covered tools like open interpreter and we recently covered open macro so we've covered a bunch of different AI agent tools now gptt me does have some similarities to some of these tools but it also has some differences so we'll quickly go over GPT me on their read me right here go over some of the features and I'm going to show you how you can set it up right now so few of the features is code execution it can read write and change files make incremental changes with the patch tool it can search and browse the web can use the browser via playright with the browser tool which is how a lot of these agents use the browser with playwright it has Vision so it can see images referenced and and prompts screenshots of your desktop and web pages and then self-correcting so output is fed back to the assistant allowing it to respond and self-correct it supports several different llm providers such as open AI anthropic open router or you can serve it locally with llama CPP so you can use pipe with this as well it has automatic naming of conventions and then optional basic web UI and rest API so to get started with this you can simply just run pip X install GPT me now I will say if you're on Windows you may have a bit of trouble trying to run this in Powers shells so I'd recommend probably using something like WSL so you can use the Linux terminal and that's what I'll be using in this video now if you're on Mac or Linux you shouldn't have any issues you can simply just run pipx install GPT me once you run that you can simply run GPT me and then this will pop up new conversation or load previous ones so you can select to either load previous ones which we don't have any right now or select a new conversation which we'll do right here so now it's saying we have no API keys so we can input an API key for open AI anthropic or open router okay for the sake of this I just use open AI so I'm going to load up that same conversation that we had here so now you can see here that it found an open AI API key and that's what we're using all right so here you'll see we can actually enter our prompt it says user here so now we can actually ask gptt me to do something right so here I'm asking what is the current time and weather in Sydney Australia Australia okay so I got an issue here you can't just actually ask it directly like that to search the um web like so you actually have to do a couple things so I went to their docks right here and I'll leave more_vertModel Thoughts Processing YouTube Transcript DataI'm now extracting structured information from a YouTube video transcript. My focus is on pulling out key points, relevant AI concepts, technical details, and any visual context flags. For non-technical videos, I'll return empty arrays for the AI concepts and technical details. I'm aiming for a comprehensive data extraction, strictly adhering to the JSON format.Analyzing Agent CapabilitiesI've been analyzing "GPT me," Josh Pook's terminal AI agent. I'm focusing on its capabilities: code execution, web browsing, and vision, including its self-correcting features. I'm noting the comparisons to other agents like Agent Q, Agent E, Open Interpreter, and Open Macro. I've noted installation steps and API requirements. Initial demos were mixed, highlighting installation snags with web browsing and a possible vision bug. Successful demos included setting up a local server and coding a Pong game.Summarizing GPT-Me FeaturesI'm now focusing on GPT me's core features. It's a terminal-based AI agent with local tool access, emphasizing file editing, web browsing (Playwright), and vision (screenshots). It supports various LLM providers and local models. I'm noting the self-correcting aspect and the built-in Flask web UI and REST API. I'm concluding that it's a developing tool, potentially behind Agent Q/Open Interpreter, but ahead of Open Macro, according to the creator's evaluation.Evaluating Multi-modal AspectsI'm now diving into the multi-modal aspects, specifically computer vision within "GPT me." I'm dissecting its autonomous task execution capabilities, focusing on the technical components: 'pipx install gptme', Playwright/Chromium, WSL support, Flask for the web UI, Python (with a Pygame example), required API keys (OpenAI, Anthropic, OpenRouter), and Llama.cpp. I've logged the visual context flags for reference and am ensuring the output remains valid JSON without any extraneous markdown. Expand to view model thoughts  chevron_right

editmore_vert{"summary": "Josh Pook reviews GPT me, a terminal-based AI agent capable of code execution, web browsing, and vision tasks. The video demonstrates the installation process, browser tool setup via Playwright, and the use of its local web UI to develop a Python game.","keyPoints": ["GPT me is a terminal-based agent that can read, write, and patch local files.","It supports web browsing using Playwright and Chromium to fetch real-time data.","The tool includes vision capabilities for interpreting desktop screenshots and web pages.","It features a Flask-based web UI and REST API for those who prefer a browser interface over the terminal.","The agent is self-correcting, meaning it analyzes error outputs and attempts to fix its own code execution."],"aiConcepts": ["AI Agents","Large Language Models (LLMs)","Self-correcting loops","Computer Vision","Multi-modal AI","Local LLM serving"],"technicalDetails": ["pipx install gptme","Playwright / Chromium","WSL (Windows Subsystem for Linux)","Flask","llama.cpp","Pygame","OpenAI/Anthropic API integration"],"visualContextFlags": [{"timestamp": 105, "reason": "CLI Setup", "context": "Shows the terminal installation process using pipx"},{"timestamp": 248, "reason": "Code Demo", "context": "Shows GPT me writing and executing Python code to fetch weather data"},{"timestamp": 425, "reason": "Web UI Demo", "context": "Demonstrates the Flask-based local web interface on localhost:5000"},{"timestamp": 505, "reason": "Implementation Demo", "context": "Shows the agent generating and launching a Pong game in a separate window"}]}thumb_upthumb_down
